name: DeFAb
description: DeFAb (Defeasible Abduction Benchmark) is a dataset and generation pipeline that converts legacy knowledge bases into defeasible theories and generates evaluation instances with polynomial-time verifiable gold-standard hypotheses. The dataset comprises 372,648+ instances across 33.75 million materialized rules from 18 knowledge sources spanning biology, law, materials science, chemistry, commonsense reasoning, biomedical informatics, and a real-time-strategy game environment. Instances are stratified into three levels: fact completion, rule abduction, and defeater abduction with formal conservativity guarantees.

sc:maintainer.sc:name: Anonymous; contact via OpenReview during review

cr:citeAs: Anonymous Authors. DeFAb: A Defeasible Abduction Benchmark and Verifier-Backed Finetuning Substrate. NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations and Datasets Track Submission. Available via anonymous mirror at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/blanc-F7E6.
rai:dataCollection: Instances are generated by a deterministic polynomial-time pipeline from publicly available knowledge bases. No crowdsourcing or human annotation is involved except for a small set (35) of high-novelty Level 3 defeaters authored by the paper's anonymous authors. Source knowledge bases are converted to definite logic programs, then to defeasible theories via a parameterized partition function. Cross-ontology extraction normalizes concept names across sources using lowercased, underscore-separated identifiers.
rai:dataCollectionRawData: Source knowledge bases include: YAGO 4.5 (CC-BY 4.0), WordNet 3.0 (Princeton License), LKIF Core (Apache 2.0), MatOnto (CC-BY 4.0), Wikidata (CC0 1.0), ConceptNet 5.8 (CC-BY-SA 4.0), Gene Ontology (CC-BY 4.0), UMLS 2025AB (NLM License, free for research), SUMO (GPLv2), FrameNet (CC-BY 3.0), MeSH (Public domain, NLM), OpenCyc (Apache 2.0), BabelNet (research license), and VisualSem (CC-BY 4.0). All are publicly available under open licenses.
rai:dataCollectionTimeframe: Source knowledge bases span 1984 (Cyc) to 2025 (UMLS 2025AB, YAGO 4.5).
rai:dataCollectionMissingData: Non-English content is filtered at the extraction stage. ConceptNet NotCapableOf edges are sparse (329 total English edges), limiting automated Level 3 defeater generation from that source.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Source knowledge bases are converted to definite logic programs, then to defeasible theories via a parameterized partition function kappa.
Cross-ontology extraction normalizes concept names across sources using lowercased, underscore-separated identifiers.
Non-English content is filtered at the extraction stage.

Synthetic contamination-control instances use invented predicate names generated by a context-free grammar over phonotactically valid syllable templates, verified for zero occurrence in Common Crawl via the infini-gram index.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: The 35 Tier 0 Level 3 instances were authored and peer-reviewed by the paper's authors using a structured rubric (anomaly explicit, hypothesis conservative, target unique). All other instances are generated automatically by the deterministic pipeline; no human labeling is required because the verifier provides ground-truth scores. Inter-author agreement on the 35 hand-authored Level 3 instances was measured via independent re-scoring (kappa = 1.0 by construction since the verifier is deterministic).
rai:dataUseCases: Evaluating foundation models on non-monotonic reasoning, belief revision, and grounded hypothesis generation.
The polynomial-time verifier-backed scoring function makes the dataset suitable as a training signal for preference optimization (DPO, RLHF, GRPO) without reward model approximation.
Contamination-resistant evaluation of formal reasoning versus memorized associations.
Multimodal (vision-language) evaluation of defeasible reasoning via the M5 modality.
Real-time verifier-gated agent deployment via the StarCraft II RTS track.
rai:dataLimitations: The base evaluation uses 409 expert-curated Tier 0 instances (35 Level 3); Wilson 95% CI half-widths are +/-15-17 pp on aggregate Level 3 accuracy.
The M1 (narrative) decoder operates approximately by design.
Automated Level 3 generation from Tier 1 remains limited by sparse ConceptNet NotCapableOf edges.
The synthetic contamination control mitigates but does not eliminate the confound: structural patterns of defeasible theories may themselves be memorized.
Defeasible rules encode general defaults and exceptions from knowledge bases, not clinical or legal guidance. Not intended for real-world scientific, legal, or medical decisions.
rai:dataBiases: Knowledge bases reflect the biases and coverage gaps of their creators and funding sources. Tier 0 domains (biology, legal reasoning, materials science) reflect expertise availability rather than representative sampling.
ConceptNet behavioral properties skew toward English-language commonsense knowledge.

Source-KB Western-academic bias: Cyc, YAGO, ConceptNet, and the biomedical ontologies are predominantly English and Western in scope; non-English commonsense and indigenous knowledge systems are under-represented.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Positive: improved evaluation of reasoning deficits may accelerate development of more reliable AI systems. Negative: low risk; formal logic benchmark with no direct path to harmful deployment. The dataset evaluates reasoning capabilities rather than producing actionable predictions.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. No personal data, human subjects, or crowdsourcing involved. All instances are derived from formal logic programs over scientific and commonsense knowledge.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: The dataset will be maintained for at least 5 years from publication by the paper's authors (de-anonymized in camera-ready). The generation pipeline enables re-generation from updated source KBs. Versioned releases follow semantic versioning (v1.0, v1.1, etc.). Tier 0 evaluation instances are frozen at v1.0 for cross-study comparability; additional tiers are additive. Bug reports and error corrections are tracked via the project repository (anonymous mirror linked under url field during review).

distribution.name: defab-anon-mirror
distribution.description: Anonymous repository mirror containing the DeFAb dataset, generation pipeline, and evaluation harness. The full repository is the distribution unit for review; SHA256 is reported by the anonymous.4open.science archive viewer.

distribution.name: tier0-instances
distribution.description: Tier 0 expert-curated evaluation instances (409 across biology, legal reasoning, materials science, plus a Level 3 set)

distribution.name: tier1-instances
distribution.description: Tier 1 cross-ontology instances across five domains from OpenCyc + ConceptNet (truncated to 50-instance samples in the review mirror; full set on de-anonymized hosting platform at camera-ready)

distribution.name: tier2-instances
distribution.description: Tier 2 multi-source domain-specific instances from Gene Ontology, UMLS, SUMO, FrameNet, Wikidata, BabelNet, MeSH (truncated to 50-instance samples per source in the review mirror)

distribution.name: tier3-instances
distribution.description: Tier 3 encyclopedic instances from YAGO 4.5 full (sub-folder under multitier in the review mirror; broken out as a separate tier in the de-anonymized release)

distribution.name: synthetic-instances
distribution.description: Synthetic contamination-control instances (409) with invented predicate names, structurally matched to Tier 0

recordSet.name: evaluation-instances
recordSet.description: Defeasible abduction evaluation instances with challenge theory, target literal, candidates, and gold-standard hypotheses. Schema is shared across all tiers (Tier 0--3 and synthetic); tier0 is used here as the canonical source for jsonPath extraction.

field.name: theory
field.description: The challenge defeasible theory (facts, strict rules, defeasible rules)

field.name: target
field.description: The target literal q to be explained

field.name: candidates
field.description: The candidate hypothesis set including distractors

field.name: level
field.description: Instance difficulty level: 1 (fact completion), 2 (rule abduction), or 3 (defeater abduction)

field.name: renderings
field.description: Instance rendered in modalities M1 (narrative), M2 (semi-formal), M3 (annotated formal), M4 (pure formal)
